hero.headline: 42 skills and expert agents for Claude Code and Codex.
hero.subheading: Automate the full development workflow — from planning and spec writing to code generation, review, and release without leaving your terminal.
hero.demoInstalled: installed 42 skills for Claude Code and Codex
hero.demoRestart: restart your tool to load the toolkit
hero.demoRestartCodex: restart Codex to load the toolkit

problemSolution.title: The workflow gets lighter when the toolkit carries the repeatable work.

before.title: Without the toolkit
items: You rewrite planning prompts and acceptance criteria for every task.
You move between scattered notes, review checklists, and release steps by hand.
You rely on general-purpose agents that do not know your development workflow.

after.title: With the toolkit
items: `ywc-plan` and `ywc-task-generator` turn rough goals into implementation-ready work.
`ywc-code-gen` and `ywc-impl-review` keep build, review, and verification loops explicit.
`/ywc-agentic` routes complex work through a capped, review-gated loop — up to three automated iterations with `ywc-impl-review` folded in before anything merges.

pipeline.title: An opinionated pipeline, not a skill grab-bag.
pipeline.description: Most of the 42 skills chain into one sequence instead of standing alone as independent prompts you have to order yourself.

steps.label: Plan
steps.description: `ywc-plan` turns a rough goal into a spec and routes it to the right size of flow.

steps.label: Converge
steps.description: `ywc-spec-ready` loops planning and validation until the spec is done, for anything beyond a small change.

steps.label: Decompose
steps.description: `ywc-task-generator` breaks the spec into dependency-safe tasks with a parallel-execution graph.

steps.label: Execute
steps.description: The executor implements each task — review, PR, and merge folded in via `--review`.

steps.label: Verify
steps.description: `ywc-gen-testcase` generates a QA test sheet per PR, ready for manual sign-off.

agentic.title: Or skip straight to `/ywc-agentic`.
agentic.description: One goal in, a capped review-gated loop out — up to three automated Plan → Execute → Evaluate → Repeat iterations with `ywc-impl-review` folded in before anything merges.

featureGrid.title: Seven categories, one pipeline, two tools.
featureGrid.description: 42 skills organized by what they do, not just how many there are.

items.label: Planning & Spec
items.description: Turn a rough goal into a validated spec or task list before any code is written.

items.label: Task & Execution
items.description: Branch, implement, verify, and merge — sequential, parallel, or fully autonomous.

items.label: Review & Verification
items.description: Architecture, security, design, and QA review, plus a self-improving learnings loop.

items.label: Git & Release
items.description: Commits, PRs, bot-review responses, and release notes handled end to end.

items.label: Toolkit & Setup
items.description: Extend the toolkit itself, configure defaults, and keep project setup consistent across tools.

items.label: Claude Code agents
items.description: Specialist read-only agents for architecture, security, performance, and language-specific review.

items.label: Codex agents
items.description: The same specialist coverage, packaged as read-only Codex agents.

Add the repository as a Codex plugin marketplace source.

items.label: License

items.label: Releases

items.question: What is this?
items.answer: `ywc-agent-toolkit` is a public toolkit of skills and custom agents for Claude Code and Codex. It helps you run repeatable development workflows from planning through release.

items.question: What are the installation requirements?
items.answer: Plugin-marketplace and Codex-plugin installation have no prerequisites. Use the commands above, then install from the matching plugin UI or marketplace.

items.question: What is the cost or token impact?
items.answer: The toolkit itself is open source and does not add a paid service. Token usage depends on how often you invoke the skills and agents in Claude Code or Codex.

items.question: Does it work with both Claude Code and Codex?
items.answer: Yes. Both tools receive 42 skills. Claude Code includes 12 custom agents, while Codex includes 7 custom agents.

items.question: Do I need to clone the repository?
items.answer: No for the paths covered on this page. Claude Code marketplace installation and Codex plugin marketplace installation are designed to install without manual cloning.

items.question: Can I keep using my existing workflow?
items.answer: Yes. The toolkit adds reusable skills such as `ywc-plan`, `ywc-code-gen`, and `ywc-impl-review`; it does not require a backend, account, or project migration.

items.question: What can the toolkit change without asking me?
items.answer: `ywc-merge-dependabot` can auto-merge dependency PRs, and `ywc-finish-branch` can merge and delete branches once its checks pass. `ywc-agentic` runs up to three automated iterations with `ywc-impl-review` folded in before anything merges. Every one of these is a skill you invoke — none of it runs unless you call it.

items.question: Do I need anything besides Claude Code or Codex?
items.answer: Plugin-marketplace installation itself has no prerequisites. Using certain skills afterward does: `python3` ≥ 3.9 (≥ 3.11 for hooks) for skills like `ywc-parallel-executor` and `ywc-finish-branch`, and the `gh` CLI for PR- and release-based skills.

items.question: Does this modify my shell or tool behavior?
items.answer: Only if you install the optional Claude Code hooks. They add guardrails like blocking dangerous shell commands and blocking access to `.env`/SSH-key files — you can skip them entirely and use only the skills.

items.question: How is this different from a generic skill collection?
items.answer: Most of the 42 skills are wired into one opinionated pipeline (plan → spec → tasks → execute → test) instead of standing alone as prompts you sequence yourself, and confirmed defects feed a review-learnings loop that tightens future reviews automatically.

items.question: Can I contribute a skill?
items.answer: Yes. `ywc-skill-author` documents the conventions, and CONTRIBUTING.md covers bug reports, new skills, and translations.
